title: scurrula notothixoides
intro: Scurrula notothixoides, also known as the poison tree or poison vine, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. It is a climber with toxic properties, containing compounds such as lignans and alkaloids, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Scurrula notothixoides is often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, and it is also used in some Ayurvedic and Unani medicine formulations.
